Transcrição Completa: 4 Sales Questions So Good Prospects Will Close Themselves
[00:00] "Most sales people talk their way out of the sale. So, what I'm going to show you today are four questions you can start using like right now with what you sell that will reframe your prospects and get them to chase you and actually close themselves."
[00:15] "Now, I want you to write this down. This is called precision probing. So, write down NPQ precision probing."
[00:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NPQ Precision Probing"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Título conceitual destacado, fundo limpo, tipografia profissional
[00:25] "Now, when are you going to use these questions? I'm going to show you. I'm going to show you why you're going to use them and then I'm actually going to role play the questions for you. So, you want to pay attention to what I'm going to go over."
[00:35] "Number one, when do you use these probing questions? When you're building what's called the gap. Now, what do I mean by building the gap? Building the gap from where the prospect is, current situation to where they want to be. What does the future look like once the newfound problems that's the gap are actually solved or emotional needs?"
[00:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Building The Gap"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Representação visual de dois pontos: "Current Situation" e "Future State" com espaço entre eles simbolizando o gap, setas indicativas
[01:00] "So, when you're building the gap, I need to probe to open them up. These are called problem awareness questions. Write that down."
[01:08] "Also, I can use probing questions when I'm future pacing. How do I get them to see what the future looks and feels like once the newfound problems are solved? Those are primarily in solution awareness questions. I might give you a few examples of those."
[01:22] "I can also use them in consequence questions that get them to see what the consequences are if they don't change. Means if they don't get their problem solved by your product or your service."
[01:33] "I can even use them in my presentation. The important part here that you have to master is why do you have to use these?"
[01:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Why Use Probing Questions?"
NÚMEROS: 3 razões listadas
VISUAL: Layout numerado com três pontos principais destacados, ícones ilustrativos ao lado de cada razão
[01:43] "Okay, now this is really important for you. Okay, do you want to make more sales than you are now, which I know you do. That's why you follow me. You might even be one of our clients. That's why you're here."
[01:53] "Why do I use these? Because I have to help the prospect relive their pain of their current situation. Now, this drives them to change. Pain drives change. You probably heard it. No pain, no gain, right? Same thing in sales. Help them relive the pain causes them to feel urgency to want to change."
[02:13] "Number two, they tell themselves why they want to change. How persuasive is it if you tell your prospect why they need to change and by what you're offering compared to you mastering how to reframe those statements into questions where the prospect tells you why they need to change, more importantly, tells themselves. Think about that. I'm going to show you some examples of that."
[02:37] "And number three causes them to go below the surface. You always hear about, 'Hey, I need to learn how to get them to open up.' Well, if we can't get them to open up, they don't feel any pain. Okay? They don't open up emotionally. They don't typically buy."
[02:52] "You've always heard this. I know you understand this. Human beings buy on what? Logic or emotion. 100% emotion. Brain studies prove that. There's no debate in that. Okay? So, this is why you want to use these questions I'm about to show you."
[03:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Emotion vs Logic"
NÚMEROS: 100% emotion
VISUAL: Comparação visual destacando "100% EMOTION" em grande destaque versus "Logic" em menor evidência, cérebro ilustrativo
[03:12] "Okay, let me show you a few examples and I want you to write this down. Of course, you can keep watching this over and over on repeat."
[03:18] "Okay, let's say a prospect, and you hear this all the time. You know what I'm talking about. Let's say a prospect gives you some type of an emotional word. Okay, so they're talking about one of their problems and they say, 'Oh my gosh, it's just causing me so much anxiety.' Or, you know, this is causing me a lot of tension at work."
[03:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Emotional Word Technique"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Palavras emocionais destacadas em balões: "Anxiety", "Tension", "Stress", "Worried", "Pressure" - layout de nuvem de palavras
[03:40] "Like if you sell to a company or this is causing I'm just worried at night you know let's say if you're selling an alarm system to a homeowner or you know oh my gosh it's just causing so much pressure for me at my job I can't figure out how to market you know get more clients if you're selling to like a small business."
[03:57] "Okay you can take that one emotional word let's say it's stressed and you can quite literally just repeat it back. So when the prospect says 'I'm just feeling a lot of stress' - stress. That's all you have to do."
[04:11] "Okay. Now, I want you not to just go practice this on your prospect today. I want you to practice on a family member or a spouse or one of your kids. When they give you any type of an emotional word, you just repeat it back. Stress or concerned or worried. Okay."
[04:26] "Now, notice how my facial expressions show that I'm what? Concerned. Like I have empathy for you. Okay. That is a concern tone. So, watch how the prospect opens up emotionally."
[04:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NEPQ Probing Question"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Destaque para metodologia NEPQ, layout profissional com logo/branding, tipografia enfatizada
[04:42] "Now, this is an example of what's called an NEPQ probing question. Now, if you're not one of our clients, you're not familiar with NPQ. NPQ stands for neuro emotional persuasion questioning. It's my methodology I developed going to school. I studied behavioral science to become a psychologist."
[05:00] "Randomly got into sales and here you are. Had an 18-year sales career, retired about a year later, started my own company as a sales trainer. Okay. All right."
[05:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "NEPQ = Neuro Emotional Persuasion Questioning"
NÚMEROS: 18 anos de carreira em vendas
VISUAL: Definição completa do acrônimo, background em psicologia comportamental destacado, timeline visual da carreira
[05:15] "Okay, so that's NPQ probe concussion. So I just got to repeat back the emotional word. Put your hand on your chest even if you're on the phone. Okay, because your body language affects your what? Your tonality. Very important for you to understand."
[05:30] "Now, okay, let's say the prospect comes back and says something like this. 'Oh, oh, stressed. Oh, yeah. I mean, just the other day it happened again' when they start going into explaining what they mean by stress."
[05:42] "Now, why does this work so well? I want to make sure you understand that when you ask this type of probing question, just repeat back an emotion, what instantly happens in your prospect's subconscious part of their brain is it basically says, 'Oh, he didn't understand what I meant by stress. I need to explain that better.' Are you getting what I'm throwing down to you?"
[06:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Subconscious Trigger"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Ilustração de cérebro com destaque para área subconsciente, setas indicando processo de pensamento automático, ícone de lâmpada para insight
[06:08] "Okay. So, they're they don't consciously do that. Subconsciously, oh, he didn't he or she didn't understand what I meant by that. I need to expand that. And that's when they start to open up."
[06:20] "Now, do you see how I'm getting them to open up emotionally? Because we're focused on a what? An emotion. Do you see what I'm talking about there? Okay, that's very important for you to understand."
[06:30] "Okay. 'Yeah. You said today it happened when' and they start expanding on that. See how I'm starting to get them to what? Relive their pain of their past history right here. Okay."
[06:42] "Now, I immediately want to ask another probing question right here. I want to find out how long that's been going on. Okay? So, they say, 'Yeah, just our day it happened when blah blah blah.' How long has it been going on for?"
[06:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Probing Question #2: Duration"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Pergunta destacada "How long has it been going on for?", ícone de calendário/relógio, linha do tempo visual
[07:00] "Now, why would I ask them how long that's been going on for? You ask them that type of question because when they say, 'Oh, it's been going on for a month or 6 months or a year,' it suddenly starts to get them to think, 'Oh my gosh, I've had the same problem for this long.'"
[07:16] "So, when the prospect tells you how long it's been going on for, you immediately come back. Let's say they said it's been going on for 6 months. 'Okay, so this has been going on for six months. Has that had a... Has that had an impact on you?'"
[07:33] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Verbal Pacing"
NÚMEROS: 6 meses (exemplo usado)
VISUAL: Destaque para técnica de pausa verbal, ondas sonoras ilustrativas mostrando ritmo lento vs rápido, ícone de velocímetro
[07:36] "Now, it's important. You're probably wondering like, why is he slowing down? What's going on? Write this down. This is called verbal pacing. All right? Most salespeople, if they ask this type of question, it doesn't land because they ask it too fast."
[07:52] "So, if I asked it like this, 'so this has been going on for the past six months, has it had an impact on you?' First of all, you're asking it too fast. So, when you ask a question too fast, it gives your prospect no time to internalize and think deeper about what you're asking."
[08:08] "So, they're going to give you a surface level knee-jerk answer. And most of the time, they'll come back like, 'Yeah, yeah, it's yeah, it's it's not been that good.' Or, 'Yeah, it's not that bad.' Because you're asking it too fast. They don't internalize it."
[08:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO (INTERRUPÇÃO PARA CTA) ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Text Jeremy Miner"
NÚMEROS: 480-637-2944
VISUAL: Número de telefone em destaque grande, ícone de celular/mensagem, call-to-action visual proeminente
[08:25] "Hey guys, Jeremy Miner here. Look, a lot of you leave comments wanting me to help you somewhere. So, the easiest way to get a hold of me, the quickest way is to text me. So text me right now. It's 480-637-2944. So 480-637-2944."
[08:42] "Listen, I started this company to help you learn how to close more deals, but do it the right way. Text me right now. Let's get back to the training video."
[08:50] "Okay, so let's say the prospect comes back and they say, 'H, I mean, you have no idea.' Okay, and they're kind of vague. What can I do? I can ask another probing question. I can lean in and say, 'In what way though?'"
[09:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Probing Question #3: Expansion"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Pergunta destacada "In what way though?", setas expandindo para fora indicando aprofundamento, ícone de lupa
[09:08] "'Oh, you have no idea, Jeremy. In what way?' Well, see, get see how I'm getting them to open up and expand. I'm probing deeper. I'm bringing up more emotion. Are you getting this? Okay."
[09:22] "All right. Let's keep going here. So, if I see if you see them and they they're emotionally opening up to you after you probe and like, 'Oh my gosh, this is so bad.' And the stress blah blah blah blah. I can immediately jump in and say, 'What's that doing to you?'"
[09:38] "Now, notice how my tone shifted. Did you feel it? What I just did there? Now, this is a role play. So, obviously, obviously, I'm showing you what to do here. In a real life sales situation, the prospect's not going to know that. Okay?"
[09:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Tone = Intention"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Equação visual destacando tom de voz = intenção percebida, ícones de ondas sonoras com expressões faciais, gráfico de empatia
[09:55] "But I'm using a concern tone there, a tone that shows empathy. Now, why is that critical for you to master? Because your tone is how the prospect interprets your intention behind everything you say and ask. That's how they interpret why you're asking."
[10:12] "So, if I ask this question, 'oh, what's it doing to you? Oh, it's it's not doing that much' unless they're lay down. But if I immediately go like this, 'what's that doing to you?' See, notice my facial expression shows that I'm concerned for you. I show empathy."
[10:30] "Now, does that lower trust that they have for you or do you feel that might raise trust if they feel like you're concerned for them? See the difference there? Okay."
[10:40] "Now, let's say the prospect says something like this. Here's another example of how to probe. All right. 'Yeah. You know, we're having some problems with XYZ and ABC.' Okay."
[10:52] "Now, a lot of sales people we see before they come into our training programs, they'll get that answer. 'We've had some problems with this and that' and they think that that's the prospect opening up by just telling them the problems and then they go through like how they solve them. This is surface level stuff. Okay?"
[11:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Surface Level vs Deep Level"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Comparação visual tipo iceberg: topo mostrando "Surface Problems" e parte submersa mostrando "Root Cause + Impact", ilustração de profundidade
[11:14] "You got to find out what's behind those problems, what's causing the problems, the root cause, and how those problems are affecting them. Even personally, even if you're selling to a Fortune 100 CEO. That's the difference. Okay?"
[11:28] "So, I need to get them to relive the pain of this, not just tell me what the problem is. Because if they tell you the problem, that's not really pain. You're only going to get the layowns this way. And I know you want to master this, don't you? Okay."
[11:43] "So, I might say, 'Can you give me a specific example of when that actually happened?' See, I'm getting them to relive their pain because now they're telling me, 'Oh, just last week or a month ago, this happened and this happened.' See how I'm drawing them back into their pain of their past history? You getting this? Okay."
[12:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Probing Question #4: Specific Example"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Pergunta destacada "Can you give me a specific example?", ícone de filme/replay, linha do tempo regressiva
[12:08] "What am I going to do there after I find out? Remember, here's the probe. 'How long has that been going on for?' Okay. 'Oh, it's been going on for about...' 'How is that affecting you personally?'"
[12:21] "Okay. 'How long has that been going on for?' Don't ask it like that. Okay. And then 'how is that affecting you personally though?' Even if I'm selling to a corporation, let's say I'm I'm selling to a department head. 'Has that had... is has that had an impact on you?' Okay, see I'm doing that now."
[12:40] "Am I going to probe and ask those feeling questions in the first two, three, four, five minutes of a conversation? Unlikely because how much trust or credibility do you have by that point? Not much. You haven't built a big enough gap."
[12:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Timing = Trust + Gap"
NÚMEROS: 2-5 minutos (tempo inicial mencionado)
VISUAL: Linha do tempo visual mostrando progressão de confiança, medidor de gap, sinal de alerta para timing prematuro
[12:58] "When I build a big enough gap, I am allowed to ask those type of feeling questions because there's more trust there because I've built a gap. Okay?"
[13:07] "Now, if you're committed to mastery or if you just got a question, text us, okay? I'll give you actually a number you can text me at that I actually respond along with some sales trainers that we have. So, 480-637-2944."
[13:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Text For Questions"
NÚMEROS: 480-637-2944, 1-2 dias úteis para resposta
VISUAL: Número de contato em destaque, ícone de mensagem, expectativa de tempo de resposta
[13:26] "Go ahead and text me any questions you have or if you're like, 'I'm not sure how to do this for my industry' and we'll get back to you. Sometimes it takes one to two business days. I just want to be realistic."
[13:37] "Oh, go ahead and hit the subscribe button if you haven't subscribed because you're probably going to want to learn what I'm going to show you in these training videos. Hope that helped today. Hit subscribe. I'll see you soon."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
100% - Percentual de decisões de compra baseadas em emoção (não lógica)
18 anos - Duração da carreira de vendas de Jeremy Miner
4 - Número de perguntas principais de probing apresentadas
480-637-2944 - Número de contato direto para dúvidas
1-2 dias úteis - Tempo de resposta para mensagens
6 meses - Exemplo de duração de problema usado no role-play
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Metodologia NEPQ (Neuro Emotional Persuasion Questioning):
Técnica de "Precision Probing" para reframe de prospects
Sistema de perguntas que fazem prospects se auto-convencerem
Baseada em ciência comportamental e psicologia
4 Perguntas de Probing Principais:
Repetição de Palavra Emocional - Repetir literalmente a emoção mencionada com tom de empatia
"How long has it been going on for?" - Fazer prospect perceber duração do problema
"In what way though?" - Expandir respostas vagas para maior profundidade
"Can you give me a specific example?" - Fazer prospect reviver a dor através de exemplos concretos
Tipos de Perguntas por Fase:
Problem Awareness Questions - Para construir o gap
Solution Awareness Questions - Para future pacing
Consequence Questions - Para mostrar impacto de não mudar
ESTRUTURA:
The Gap Framework:
Situação Atual → Gap (Problemas não resolvidos) → Estado Futuro Desejado
Construção progressiva de confiança antes de perguntas profundas
Timing baseado em nível de rapport estabelecido
Verbal Pacing Technique:
Desacelerar deliberadamente ao fazer perguntas importantes
Pausas estratégicas para permitir internalização
Evitar perguntas rápidas que geram respostas superficiais
3 Razões para Usar Probing Questions:
Fazer prospect reviver a dor da situação atual (urgência)
Fazer prospect se auto-convencer da necessidade de mudança
Fazer prospect ir além da superfície e abrir-se emocionalmente
Princípios de Tonalidade:
Tom = Como prospect interpreta sua intenção
Tom de preocupação/empatia aumenta confiança
Linguagem corporal afeta tonalidade (mesmo ao telefone)
Expressões faciais devem refletir empatia genuína
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Não foram mencionados valores específicos de vendas ou ROI neste vídeo de treinamento
DURAÇÃO: ~13:45 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional de treinamento em vendas com role-plays demonstrativos
